messages.subject: [R-jobs] Opening for a Research Statistician
messages.body_plain:
The Fraunhofer Institute for Industrial Mathematics ITWM in
Kaiserslauern, Germany, is looking for a researcher in its
department for Financial Mathematics, starting as soon as
possible.

We are a team working in the following domains of research
and industrial projects: Development of models and algorithms
for pricing of financial derivatives; evaluation, optimization
and risk assessment of portfolios; statistical analysis of
operational risks as well as detection of peculiarities in
large data sets.

In particular, we search for colleagues graduated in a mathematical
degree programme (master or diploma), with profound knowledge in
statistics, experience in exploratory data analysis, and programming
skills---preferably in R, but otherwise also in Matlab or C#---and
with proficiency in English and German of the following form:

      Understanding         Speaking                Writing
      Listening  Reading    Spoken      Spoken
                            interaction production
English: C1-C2    C1-C2       C1         C1           C1
German:  B2-C1    B2          B1         B2           B2

according to the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages

        http://www.coe.int/t/dg4/linguistic/cadre_en.asp

For details see our opening at:

        https://jobs.fraunhofer.de/Vacancies/78607/Description

or directly contact

Dr. Peter Ruckdeschel, peter.ruckdeschel at itwm.fraunhofer.de  or
Dr. J?rg Wenzel, joerg.wenzel at itwm.fraunhofer.de

Best regards,
Peter Ruckdeschel

messages.subject: [R-jobs] DC area: International Food Policy Research Institute looking for an Infographist/Technical Writer
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] DC area: International Food Policy Research Institute looking for an Infographist/Technical Writer
messages.body_plain:
POSTING DATE: JUNE 9, 2011
CLOSING DATE: JUNE 30, 2011, OR UNTIL FILLED

The International Food Policy Research Institute (IFPRI) seeks to recruit a
Technical Writer into its HarvestChoice team in the Environment and
Production Technology Division. This is a one-year, exempt, renewable
appointment depending on performance and funding based at IFPRI headquarters
in Washington, D.C. HarvestChoice is mandated to generate, disseminate and
enhance the capacity to utilize strategic information on investment options
for raising smallholder productivity and market participation in sub-Saharan
Africa. HarvestChoice is jointly implemented with the Center for
International Science and Technology Policy and Practice (InSTePP) at the
University of Minnesota, and cross-team collaboration in achieving the goals
of HarvestChoice is an important source of synergy and innovation in the
initiative. The Technical Writer will work under the day-to-day supervision
of IFPRI HarvestChoice team leader.

Essential Duties:

- The successful candidate will play a key role in contributing to and
developing the documentation of the data, tools and analyses generated by
the IFPRI HarvestChoice team. Such documents include data and method
technical notes, working papers, and investment briefs
- Managing the web content pipeline of the team IFPRI onto the HarvestChoice
website, preparation of documents in publishable format, and working in
close collaboration with divisional and institute communication specialists
ensuring HarvestChoice outputs are appropriately promoted and disseminated
(including the use of social media).

Required Qualifications:

- A minimum of Bachelor's degree in either an agriculture or environment
related discipline or in technical writing/communications with a strong
track record of developing scientific or technical documentation.
- At least 3 years of post graduation work experience in a relevant position
with clearly demonstrated contribution to a number of technical
publications.
- Strong writing skills in English.
- Ability to communicate complex research results in a concise and engaging
fashion, in both verbally and written
- High level of proficiency in the use of document preparation and sharing
software tools in networked and web-based environments, and experience in
the use of social media tools.
- Proficient in generating clear and compelling graphics and tables from
large data files and in the use of desktop and web publishing or similar
publication-ready document software
- Highly output-focused and conscientious with a strong attention to detail.
- Ability to work under pressure in an international team setting.

Preferred Qualifications:

- Proficiency in the use of GIS software to annotate and publish maps
- Proficiency in French
- Data management skills in Excel and MySQL
- Experience in generating infographics

TO APPLY:

External Applicants: Go to www.ifpri.org . Click on "Careers" and then click
on "Research" to link you to Position # 11-166- Technical Writer, EPTD.
Please complete on-line application, including a complete resume and a
detailed letter of interest. Also complete section providing 3 references.
For more information on IFPRI, please visit our website at www.ifpri.org.

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Graphics software developer job at NIST
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Graphics software developer job at NIST
messages.body_plain:
This is a term contractor position at the National Institute of
Standards and Technology, in Gaithersburg, Maryland.  The position is 
ready to be filled now.

A candidate is needed to develop software for the interactive creation and 
editing of phase equilibrium diagrams (2 dimensional plots with 
annotations). The candidate must have expertise in use of widget toolkits 
and graphical APIs for the development of GUI applications. The candidate 
must be able to develop code using Java (including Java 2D graphics) or 
JavaScript / DOM / HTML Canvas. Experience with algebraic transformations, 
curve fitting, rendering, interpolation, intersection, and collision/hit 
detection of curves, splines, and 2-D objects is preferred.

To be considered, please send a statement of interest and a CV to Terrell 
Vanderah 
(http://www.nist.gov/mml/ceramics/structure_determination/terrell-vanderah.cfm).

messages.subject: [R-jobs] data scientist/R programmer with focus on location data
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] data scientist/R programmer with focus on location data
messages.body_plain:
Sense Networks is looking for a data scientist with a thorough background in
applied statistics, data visualization and exploration.
Our data are typically location-referenced so experience in working with
spatial data is a great plus.
Having worked with "big" data on multiple server architectures is another
valuable asset.
We are looking for candidates who pay attention to details but at the same
time are skilled in rapid prototyping.
The candidate should be sufficiently experienced and methodical in software
design to make results easily reproducible.
We need a versatile and motivated contributor who can design and lead a work
flow which starts from noisy data and ends in meaningful insights.

Candidates for this position should:
- have experience in modern regression, classification and categorical data
analysis
- thoroughly understand all core statistical concepts
- be skilled in designing efficient algorithms
- have experience in visualizations of spatiotemporal data
- have experience in condensing large data sets into various views and
hierarchically organized summaries.

Requirements
- M.S.  in statistics or computer science or related field with 2+ years
experience
- Experience with a statistical package is required, preferably R, as well
as a basic understanding of data architecture.
- HIGH level of comfort with the Linux environment and its various tools
- Knowledge of Java is a plus.

This is a great opportunity to work in a dynamic team in our Manhattan
office. This will be a consulting arrangement but could potentially lead to
a long term relationship.
We are looking to fill this position as soon as possible.

Please send your resume and cover letter to
info at sensenetworks.com

Thanks,
Markus Loecher
Lead Scientist
Sense Networks

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-jobs] post-doc fellow in Regensburg, Germany
messages.body_plain:
We have an opening for a* post-doc researcher* in *Statistical*
*Bioinformatics*


Applicants are expected to pursue independent research in a topic
related to one of the research activities in the group. These include
biological network modelling, genomic data integration, and the analysis
of high dimensional genomic data including microarray, metabolomics and
next generation sequencing data. In general, we are open to the
introduction of new topics into the group, as long as they fit our profile.

*Applicants should have** *a very good PhD, in bioinformatics, computer
science, statistics, biology, physics or a related field , experience in
data analysis/modelling, experience in programming, excellent
presentation and writing skills, and a strong commitment to pursuing a
research career.


The Spang group conducts research in various areas of medicine related
bioinformatics including computational method development as well as
applied bioinformatics in numerous cooperations with biomedical
researchers. It currently consists of 13 full-time researchers
(graduates or postdocs) from six countries. Please also see our recent
publications for an overview of our activities.

The position is paid according to German TV?D-13 and will be filled for
an initial period of *two years* but can be prolonged.


Send applications containing the usual documents including a brief
statement of your research interests by email to *Prof. Rainer Spang*
(rainer.spang at ur.de).


The University of Regensburg is an equal-opportunity employer.
Applications from disabled persons are, if equally qualified, handled
with preference. Qualified woman are strongly encouraged to apply.





-- 
-----------------------------------------
Julia C. Engelmann
~ Statistical Bioinformatics ~
Institute for Functional Genomics
University of Regensburg
Josef-Engert-Str. 9 (Biopark I)
93053 Regensburg, Germany
phone:  +49 941 943 5052
fax:    +49 941 943 5020
julia.engelmann at klinik.uni-regensburg.de



	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
